name: Río Cerezuelo Circular Trail
name_original: Cascada de la Malena y castillo de la Yedra - circular trailrunning por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
description: Challenging 3.0-mile (4.8 km) jogging route along Río Cerezuelo with 802 feet (244 metres) elevation gain, passing La Yedra Castle.
region_intro: Jogging around Cazorla offers an exceptional opportunity to explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The region features an extensive network of trails winding through mountainous terrain, river valleys, and dense forests. Runners can experience varied challenges, from soft soil paths to rocky ascents, with significant elevation changes across the park. Best jogging routes around Cazorla The most popular jogging route is Río Cerezuelo Circular Trail, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 45 minutes to complete. This route follows the Cerezuelo River through shaded areas and a narrow gorge, featuring flowing waters and lush greenery. Another top favourite among local runners is Puente Herrerías - Poyos de la Mesa Trail, a difficult 13.9 miles (22.4 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from elevated areas like Los Poyos de la Mesa, rewarding those who tackle its ascents. Local runners also love the Pósito Trail and Río Guadalquivir Loop, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through the lush setting near the source of the Guadalquivir River, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Jogging around Cazorla is defined by its mountainous terrain, picturesque river trails, and extensive forests. The region offers a wide range of running routes suitable for various fitness levels, from moderate paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Cazorla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Cazorla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cazorla?
A: Cazorla offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 20 trails available for you to explore. These routes traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Cazorla?
A: The jogging trails in Cazorla feature highly diverse terrain, ranging from soft soil paths to rocky ascents. You'll encounter mountainous areas, picturesque river valleys, and dense forests, providing varied challenges and scenic backdrops for your runs.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Cazorla?
A: While many routes in Cazorla are considered challenging, there are some moderate options that can be suitable for beginners looking for a rewarding experience. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367475091/posito-trail-and-rio-guadalquivir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pósito Trail and Río Guadalquivir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.3-mile (11.7 km) trail that leads through a lush setting near the source of the Guadalquivir River.

Q: Can I find running routes with waterfalls in Cazorla?
A: Yes, Cazorla is home to several stunning waterfalls that can be incorporated into your running adventures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40303297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerezuelo River Trail – Malena Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4473525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malena Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls in the region include the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2613975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de los Órganos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1062504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linarejos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cazorla?
A: Absolutely! Circular routes are popular in Cazorla, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A fantastic example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989160640/rio-cerezuelo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cerezuelo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 3.0-mile (4.8 km) path following the Cerezuelo River through shaded areas and a narrow gorge.

Q: What are some of the best viewpoints or landmarks to see while running in Cazorla?
A: Cazorla offers breathtaking views and interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989196142/puente-herrerias-poyos-de-la-mesa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Herrerías - Poyos de la Mesa Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic vistas from elevated areas like Los Poyos de la Mesa. You can also explore the lush setting near the source of the Guadalquivir River or visit the historic La Yedra Castle for commanding views of the village.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Cazorla?
A: The running routes in Cazorla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from river trails and waterfalls to dense forests and challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunity to connect with nature in this rich wildlife area.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or offer refreshing waterside runs?
A: Yes, river trails are a highlight of running in Cazorla. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989160640/rio-cerezuelo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cerezuelo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is known for its shaded areas, flowing waters, and lush greenery. The Borosa River Trail is another popular option, offering stunning landscapes and winding paths through a narrow gorge.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while running in Cazorla?
A: The Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park is one of Andalusia's richest areas for wildlife. Running here offers an excellent chance to spot various species like deer, wild boar, and numerous birds in their natural habitat, enhancing your connection with the environment.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options or trail networks for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a significant challenge, Cazorla offers extensive options. The GR 247 Bosques del Sur trail network is the longest circular hiking trail in Spain, spanning 317 kilometers with additional variants, providing endless possibilities for long-distance trail running. Many routes also feature substantial elevation gain, requiring good fitness and sturdy footwear.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for running routes in Cazorla?
A: Running routes in Cazorla vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989160640/rio-cerezuelo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cerezuelo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 3.0 miles (4.8 km) and takes around 45 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cazorla – Cazorla loop&lt;/a&gt;, can be up to 12.0 miles (19.3 km) and take several hours to complete, depending on your pace and the elevation.
tips:
La Yedra Castle is an ancient defensive structure in the Spanish municipality of Cazorla. It is located at the foot of the Salvatierra hill, above the Cerezuelo river, in the province of Jaén. It is a building from the Berber period, but it was completed in its current form by the Castilians. From the complex, located 831 meters above sea level, you can see the city of Cazorla and its surroundings.

The architecture has several Muslim features, although over the centuries Christians completed the final design, so the Gothic style predominates (translated from Wikipedia).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Río Cerezuelo Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied experience with both paved and unpaved sections. You'll encounter natural paths, gravel, and asphalt. Be prepared for some uneven, rocky, and steep segments, especially after leaving the river. Conditions can be muddy or wet, particularly if the river is high or after rain. The route features well-defined paths, handrails, and charming wooden bridges crossing the river multiple times.

faqs.question: How challenging is this trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: While some sections are easily accessible, the trail is rated as difficult for jogging due to its mixed terrain and elevation changes. Some parts can be demanding with steep inclines and uneven surfaces. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness, especially if you plan to tackle the full loop or extend your run to more challenging sections.

faqs.question: What notable sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy breathtaking views of the Cerezuelo River, lush riverside forests, and the Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park. Key features include charming wooden bridges, waterfalls like Cascada de la Malena and La Escaleruela, and spectacular views of the historic town of Cazorla, including the iconic La Yedra Castle. The landscape also transitions to open areas with terraced cultivation and limestone walls.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is easily accessible directly from the historic town of Cazorla. You should look for parking options within or on the outskirts of Cazorla, as the trail begins close to the town center. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors to the natural park and town attractions.

faqs.question: Is the Río Cerezuelo Circular Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Río Cerezuelo Circular Trail itself or the general areas of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park for day visits. However, always check local regulations for any specific activities or protected zones you might encounter.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly recommended for its vibrant foliage and pleasant temperatures. Spring also offers lush greenery and comfortable conditions. Summers can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon jog is advisable. Winters can bring cooler temperatures and potentially muddy conditions, especially if there's been rain.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other official trails. You'll find it intersects with the Río Cerezuelo trail for almost its entire length, and also passes through significant portions of the De Ermitas y Monasterios, Variante 3 (1/2), and El Gilillo trails.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends directly in the historic town of Cazorla, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and other facilities readily available before or after your jog. This makes it convenient for refreshments and amenities.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners who want to try jogging?
faqs.answer: While some sources describe parts of the trail as low difficulty, the full Río Cerezuelo Circular Trail is rated as difficult for jogging due to its mixed and sometimes challenging terrain, including steep sections. Beginners should be prepared for a demanding workout and consider starting with shorter, less strenuous sections or building up their fitness before attempting the full loop.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of the Cerezuelo River to the area?
faqs.answer: The Cerezuelo River has historically been central to the life of Cazorla's inhabitants. It played a vital role in powering mills and irrigating orchards, shaping the local economy and landscape for centuries. The trail offers a unique opportunity to experience this close relationship between the town and its natural water source.
